segment-text: Zach Braff & Adam Braff The SOUND OF A MAN PANTING... FOOTSTEPS racing, TWIGS SNAPPING underfoot... His breath is heavy to feed his pace.
INT. FOREST - MORNING

TIGHT on AIDAN BLOOM (35) as he sprints through the ethereal morning fog of a BEAUTIFUL FOREST. He wears a FUTURISTIC SPACE SUIT and HELMET and clutches a WARRIOR'S BROAD SWORD in his fist. He squints his eyes as he looks back at SOMETHING unseen and ominous that's CHASING HIM. Aidan hears a NOISE. He looks up to see A SMALL FLYING ROBOTIC UNIT hovering alongside him. It beeps like R2D2 as if to say "It's gaining on us!" AIDAN's FRANTIC FACE; he's sprinting with every ounce of energy he has when...
Dad, what's your password?
ON AIDAN: Where the fuck did that come from? TUCKER BLOOM (6) shirtless and carrying an electric drill and an ipad steps out from behind a GIANT REDWOOD.
Dad! What's your password?!
Aidan stops abruptly and stares at his son; confused.
INT. BLOOM HOUSE KITCHEN TABLE - MORNING

ON AIDAN'S FACE: the CAMERA PULLS BACK QUICKLY to reveal Aidan at the head of his family's chaotic morning breakfast routine. The TV plays EARSPLITTING CARTOONS. Aidan's wife SARAH BLOOM (33) chops carrots for lunches in the background. Dad!!!???
I have no fucking idea! Swear jar.
Fuck the swear jar.
Aidan.
I'm sorry, but fuck the swear jar.
(singing) You gotta fuck the swear jar...
Tucker! Stop. The password is (to Aidan) What are you doing?
The swear jar is full. Look at it.
We see an OVERFLOWING SWEAR JAR on top of the refrigerator.
The swear jar is all the money we have to send you kids to college so you should be happy that I curse so that you and your sister can one day blow off class and pull bong hits in your dorm room.
What's a bong?
It's a type of vase.
What's a vase?
A bong for flowers.
GRACE (12) enters. She is dressed in all black with long sleeves and a skirt that goes down to her ankles.
I heard you, dad. Rabbi Perlman says cursing is for the weak minded.
Yeah well Rabbi Perlman's breath makes God question why he even bothered creating the universe.
If you can curse we all can curse. Fine.
Hairy balls. Gracie!
Wow! I did not see that coming. Way to dive in.
Tucker holds up the ipad to show a GORGEOUS FERRARI.
Dad, we need this car.
We're not getting a Ferrari, but right now you can curse.
Fuck and tits.
Went for kind of a combo there, I like it.
This Ferrari is the coolest car in the whole universe.
It amazes me how clueless you people seem to be about our financial situation. Do you want me to sell the minivan?
Yes!
Well I'm not going to. It has more beverage caddies than any other embarrassing car in its class.
That's fucking bullshit!
How is this helpful?
Who cares if they curse? It's so stupid. I never understood what the big deal is.
There is a societal agreement that children don't curse.
Well I disagree with society's agreement. And right now, at this particular moment; I don't give a flying fuck if anyone in this house curses.
Fuck everybody. Anuses!
Sarah can't help but laugh. She's too exhausted to care.
Shit. Clock. School!
EXT. BLOOM HOUSE - MOMENTS LATER
Aidan bursts through the front door in a nice suit. Grace and Tucker are dressed in conservative private school uniforms. Sarah comes rushing out of the house...
Tucker! Yalmulka. Tzitzit.
Sarah brings a Jewish "skullcap" and tzitzit (a knotted, fringe worn by religious Jewish men and boys) to the open mini-van door.
What is wrong with you, man?
I was thinking about that Ferrari.
Forget about the Ferrari. It's time for you to start thinking about the plight of the Jews.
What does plight mean?
Ask the Rabbis, that's what we're paying them for.
You have to talk to your father today, Aidan. He still hasn't paid this semesters tuition.
Uchh, I hate talking to him about money. When was it due?
A month ago. They're threatening to kick them out if they don't get a check by the end of the week.
Ok. I'll talk to him today.
Aidan backs the car out of the driveway.
(calling out) Avoid the rabbis if you see them!
(with a smile) I always do!
They pull away.
INT. MINIVAN - MOMENTS LATER
An AMAZING SONG plays on the car stereo as Tucker struggles to redress his tzitzit under his shirt in the moving car.
You need help gearing up back there, Yentl?
Don't call me Yentl.
You can call me Yentl. I love that movie.
Unfortunately we know.
(singing) Papa can you hear me?
Please don't do that.
She reaches for the stereo.
If you change this song I will eat your fingers off.
The boys get to do all the cool things to honor Hashem. I won't get to do anything remotely cool until
I buy my sheitel.
Sheitel? What's that again?
It's a modesty wig. When I get married I'll shave my head and wear a wig for the rest of my life. That way only my husband will find me pretty.
No offense, but that's the weirdest tradition I've ever heard. And it wouldn't even work on you. You're so pretty, you'd look even prettier with a shaved head. It'll defeat the whole purpose.
Grace can't help but smile at that. Aidan sees something.
You've got to be fucking kidding me. What?
GRACE
He pulls the car over. A LARGE CONCRETE RETAINING WALL near their home has been covered with GRAFFITI. It appears to be a 1950'S ERA ROBOT.
Who did that?
A douchebag that's who. And you know what? That's gonna be there for years! The city's bankrupt; they're never gonna get around to covering that up.
Dad, we're gonna be late for morning prayers.
Aidan shakes his head and floors the minivan down the street.
INT. MINIVAN / EXT. YESHIVA - MOMENTS LATER

The minivan door flies open in front of a Yeshiva (a very religious Jewish private school). Conservative looking parents and their children intermingle with RABBIS in full regalia (black suits, hats, long curled sideburns.)
Wait Tucker, come back here...
Aidan leans into the back seat to tuck in Tucker's shirt and make sure his tzitzit are hanging correctly.
We don't want you to get in trouble like last time. And stop chewing on those knots like you're a Rottweiler; these things are expensive. (then) The drill.
Tucker pulls the electric drill from his backpack.
Good. Now go be Jewish. Why?
I'm not sure; ask the Rabbis.

The second the door closes Aidan digs into his ash tray to pull out a half-smoked joint. He lights it as he pulls away. KNOCK, KNOCK, KNOCK! Aidan whips his head to reveal RABBI ROSENBERG in full Orthodox regalia with the addition of a NEON ORANGE CROSSING GUARD VEST knocking on his window. Aidan quickly stabs out the roach and rolls down the window.
Shalom, Mr. Bloom.
Shalom, Rabbi Rosenberg.
Mr. Bloom, Rabbi Twersky would like to see you in his office if you have a moment.
Oh, you know, today I really can't. Today, believe it or not, a moment is not something I have. Even for a man as mighty and all-knowing as Rabbi Twersky. I'm gonna have to take a rain-check.
Speaking of rain, as you know every year to celebrate the holiday of Tu Bishvat we ask the children to donate money to have a tree planted in Israel.

Yeah I heard about that. But when you think about it, how many trees do they really need over there, Rabbi? Besides, I gave like a forest at my Bar Mitzvah. Right now I need to focus on my own little holy-land over in Reseda. You know; times being what they are...
Which is why, Mr. Bloom, we encourage the children to plant a seedling in their own backyard.
Rabbi Rosenberg leans down and pulls a completely DEAD EVERGREEN SEEDLING out of a cardboard box.
You never picked up your seedling, Mr. Bloom.
He hands it through the car window.
Wow, thank you. I'm gonna go do some CPR on this thing the second I have absolutely nothing to do.
Just try not to smoke it.
He gives Aidan a wry smile.
Funny. Clever. Have any of the other rabbis told you that you look very sexy in neon.
Talk to Rabbi Twersky about the tuition; don't make it uncomfortable for the children. (beat) Times being what they are... Right.
(big smile) Baruch Hashem.
Yeah, bless God... bless everybody.
On Aidan's face as he pulls away...
INT. MINIVAN - MOMENTS LATER
Aidan pulls the minivan over to the side of the road. He stares at something off-screen. He shakes his head.
(to himself) There were three gunshots. There were three gunshots. One. Two.
He speeds off. The camera pans 180 degrees to REVEAL he was staring at the GRAFFITI COVERED RETAINING WALL.
INT. HARDWARE STORE - DAY
Aidan, still in his suit, fills a shopping cart with painting supplies: a paint roller on a long pole, paint cans, etc.
(to himself) We've heard that from you time and time again over the many weeks that we've been assembled here.
A CONTRACTOR passes and stares at Aidan talking to himself.
EXT. CONCRETE RETAINING WALL - LATER

INSERT: THE ROBOT'S FACE on the wall as a paint roller rolls over his body erasing him forever into the grey nothingness of municipal infrastructure. WE POP BACK TO REVEAL: Aidan, stripped down to an undershirt, but with his suit pants still on; painting over the graffiti.
And now you have the audacity to insult everyone in this room by saying they might not have been gunshots?
Aidan takes a step back to admire his work. He turns to his REFLECTION in the minivan window.
(to himself in reflection) What were they then, Mr. Ortega?
He raises his fingers in the shape of a gun at his reflection and pulls his pointer finger trigger.
INT. CASTING AGENCY, WAITING ROOM - LATER
A WIDE SHOT reveals Aidan sitting amongst a room full of AFRICAN AMERICAN MEN dressed in sharp suits. CLOSE UP on one of the ACTORS.
(to himself) BANG!
CLOSE UP on a SECOND ACTOR holding his finger gun sideways. BANG!
INT. AUDITION ROOM - MOMENTS LATER
CLOSE ON AIDAN. Through this speech WE PULL BACK TO REVEAL the SIX PRODUCERS behind a desk watching him.

(He does this well:) Three bangs you said. Bang, bang, bang. This entire case is dependant on your testimony that on the night of January 7th, outside your food truck you heard three shots. Well now Mr. Ortega, you only have one shot. One shot to tell us who has threatened you with a retribution so terrifying that you'd be willing to change your entire testimony and let an assassin free to hunt down more little boys like Anthony Jackson. You've got a cross around your neck... I ask you, sir... How many "bangs" did Jesus hear?

REVEAL the bewildered expressions on the faces of the Producers. A few whisper to each other. WE SEE A CLOSE-UP of Aidan's nervous vulnerability through the MONITOR of the CAMCORDER FILMING his audition.
Aidan, that was very good as always. But we asked you to come in for the other role; Mr. Ortega.

Yeah, I know. And thank you for calling me in... but that role only has one line. And plus... Ortega is Hispanic. I mean don't get me wrong, I could tan and really go for it; but I really feel like I got a solid handle on this prosecutor.

Aidan we've already decided to go Black with that role. You probably noticed that you were the only white person in the waiting room. And truthfully we're only seeing those men out of courtesy. We just offered the role to Taye Diggs and he's accepted.
Oh. Wow. I feel really stupid now. I'm just trying to be as proactive as possible.
I understand. We'd still love you to read Mr. Ortega's line...

Oh... yeah... sure. I mean I get it... If you're going Black, you're going Black. I can get Ortega tan, but certainly not Taye Diggs tan... Unless I had those pills C. Thomas Howell ate in the movie "Soul Man". Did you ever see that movie?
They stare at him. One PRODUCER puts his head in his hand.
Well don't. It's totally racist. Or maybe it isn't. I don't know. Maybe I only think it's racist cause I'm white and I feel guilty laughing.
Ask Taye on set. I'm sure Taye's
We're ready when you are Aidan.
Aidan takes a deep breath. Shakes the stress out of his body.
(Bad Hispanic accent) For the last time, I no longer think that what I heard were gunshots. Probably just a car backfiring.
Thank you, Aidan.
OFF AIDAN's DEFEATED HALF SMILE...
INT. MINIVAN - DAY
Aidan blasts a GREAT SONG as he flies down the freeway. His face shows his misery. Fuck!!!
INT. BLOOM HOUSE - AIDAN'S OFFICE - LATER
Aidan masturbates while standing to a PORN CLIP on his computer screen. His monitor hides the action.
with that thick Black cock!
Aidan whips around in shock to reveal his father, SAUL BLOOM (75) he is eating peanuts out of a jar.
Dad, what the fuck?!
Yeah, hit me with it. Hit me in the head with it.
Aidan scrambles to stop the VIDEO. Saul seems unfazed.
You know Genesis tells us to never spill our seed.
Why would Phil Collins care if I masturbate?
The book of the Bible, not the music group.
I'm equally uninterested in what either has to say.
Trouble in the bedroom, Aidan? Men have man-needs. Those man-needs need to be met or you end up taking things into your own hands.
Dad, why haven't you paid the yeshiva tuition? I've got aggro rabbis stalking me in crosswalks.
It's like an Orthodox Zombie movie.
We need to talk. But can you please clean yourself up? I can't talk to you with your pants at your ankles. I'll meet you outside. Fine.
Saul starts to leave, then turns back.
You're not going to return to masturbating are you?
No! Dad, get out!
Ok. Cause I have to be at the hospital at five and I don't wanna get stuck in traffic on the 101.
Dad, get out!
He does. Aidan shakes his head; incredulous. Then turns back to the monitor, thinks about it for a beat, then hits play and continues masturbating.
It's so much! It's so much!
EXT. BLOOM HOUSE - DECK - LATER

Aidan dries his hands with a dish towel as he crosses to find his father standing at the precipice of a HALF-FINISHED DECK. The frame has been laid for something much larger, but the boards have only been installed on half of it. A GOLDEN RETRIEVER (KUGEL) takes a giant shit in the corner of the deck.
Kugel, no. (to Aidan) Sorry, I know you can't stand the dog; he's usually so well trained.
If you trained that dog to shit everywhere but the lawn, then you've done an incredible job.
Deck looks good.
Was that sarcasm or serious? I couldn't tell.
I guess it was half-sarcastic; for the half that isn't finished. (then) And you better have that fence fixed before it falls on one of the kids.

Well as I'm sure you know quite well by now, Dad; we don't have any money. Sarah's working her ass off, I'm auditioning every chance I get, but I haven't gotten a decent check since that dandruff commercial.
And ironically you still have dandruff.

What is going on with the kids' tuition? We had a deal; you said you'd cover it if you got to pick the school. You got your wish. they're going to the Yeshiva; you get to have your brainwashed grandkids. But come on; you can't make me hound you for these checks.
The cancer's back, Aidan. Oh my God.
I didn't want you to worry you until I knew for sure...
But now you do.
Now I do and it's pretty bad.
How bad is pretty bad?
It's all over my lungs. Oh my God.
Funny how even you atheists call for Hashem when there's nowhere else to turn.
Once again, I'm an agnostic not an atheist; I'll believe it when I see it.

Well you may just have a chance. I've decided to try an experimental drug trial. They're trying this new protocol that's shown some very promising results but I'm gonna have to pay out of pocket. So...
So I can't afford the school anymore, Aidan. I'm down to my last savings and I've decided to spend it on this. So you win.
My father has cancer, my kids have to drop out of school and I don't have a job. How am I winning?
God works in mysterious ways.
This one's particularly mysterious.
Look I'm gonna be layed up for awhile. I'm gonna need you to watch Kugel.
Aidan has to sit down to catch his breath.
Okay, slow down. This is a lot of horrible news at once... (then) What do we do?
What do you mean what do we do? We move forward. It's the only direction God gave us...
OFF AIDAN'S SHOCKED FACE...
INT. DARK ROOM - DAY

CLOSE UP ON A TWITTER PAGE: WE SEE that whomever is typing is going through names of famous celebrities and telling them to "FUCK OFF AND DIE" one by one. eg: @tomcruise FUCK OFF AND DIE, @mileycyrus FUCK OFF AND DIE, @davidhasselhoff FUCK OFF AND DIE A LOUD KNOCK at the door. The CAMERA PULLS BACK FAST to reveal JONAH BLOOM (29) as he whips his head to the door. HE sits hunched over his computer in a tiny dark room.
Go away!
JONAH
Dude, I drove all the way out here open the fucking door.
What's the password?
Is it "My brother once caught me fucking a Winnie-the-Pooh stuffed animal."?
It was Eeyore and I was eight; fuck off.
Oh, I just remembered the password. It's: "Your father's dying and I need to talk to you. (MORE)
So take a break from trolling the internet for two seconds and open the door, you lazy, miserable fuck."
Jonah stands and opens the door as BRIGHT SUNLIGHT STREAMS into his room to reveal WE ARE:
EXT. AIRSTREAM TRAILER - MALIBU TRAILER PARK - CONTINUOUS 15
Jonah lives in a trailer park on a cliff in Malibu. The setting is 180 degrees different from his dark lair.
I knew that was it. (an introduction) Sunlight, Jonah. Jonah, sunlight.
Is Dad really dying again or are you just crying wolf?
It's all over his lungs, Jonah. The wolf is here.
They watch as Kugel takes a shit on a neighbors Astroturf. Kugel!
No, don't stop him. I can't fucking stand that guy. We'll call that a little thank you gift for doing crunches to Nicky Minaj at 6am.
Your can looks good.
You know when you make fun of the trailer, you make fun of my inheritance from Mom. She wanted me to live how I choose. And be free to pursue the goals that I have.
Yeah. What are those goals again? Weren't you gonna develop some iPhone app or something?
Fuck apps; saturated market. I'm gonna start blogging. Get some of that ad revenue. Gonna call it:
"Fucking Shit I Fucking Hate."

Sounds like web-crack for the miserable. Look I don't care if you sit in there all day searching the web for strangers to hate. But I have two major problems and one I think you might be able to help me with. What?
Fuck off.
JONAH
I can't watch this dog and take care of two kids and go to auditions, Jonah.
Absolutely not.
Dude are you fucking kidding me? You've got a rally with me. This is your father we're talking about.
So when you get old and sick everyone's just supposed to forget that you were an asshole?
Look, I know he's not easy. He lost it a little when Mom died. We all did. It's time to move on.
What's the other one? What?
You said there were two problems. I'll help with the one that's not that dog.
The other one's that Dad's gonna try an experimental treatment this time. He can't afford the yeshiva for the kids anymore. Mazel Tov.
Well, they're not going to Jefferson. It's ranked one of the worst in the state.
Just because you had it rough doesn't mean they will.
I didn't have it rough.
Whatever you need to tell yourself. Sounds like you got your hands full, buddy. Live long and prosper.
Are you serious?
I have to go back to work. Don't you dare leave that dog.
He goes back inside. Aidan waits a beat then quietly ties the dog to a nearby shaded tree. He tosses out a neighbor's plant, fills the pot with water and sets it beside Kugel.
Can I say something that's probably going to piss you off?
INT. VIDEO RENTAL STORE - DAY
Aidan, Sarah, Grace and Tucker stroll the aisles. They are the only ones in the store. The shelves are almost barren.
I can't wait.
You could home school them.
Are you out of your fucking mind?
I thought we were discussing all options. You're a stay-at-home-dad; is home schooling not one of our options?
What are we fucking Amish?
Look online there's a whole new movement out there. Parents are considering other options. (then) Think about how well you'd get to know the kids.
I know the kids just fine.
Dad, can we get this?
Aidan looks at it. It looks like soft-core porn.
There's tits right on the cover of the box, man. Why would I rent this for you?
It's about nurses.
I see that. Go look for some animated bullshit. Who's that Hispanic chick you like who's always up in everybody's business?
Dora. He hates Dora.
I hate Dora.
Well go find something that doesn't have tits on the cover, man. (MORE)
Come on, you want me to get arrested? (beat, to Sarah) If you want me to give up acting you should just say it.
I think you should give up acting.
I'm not giving up acting.
We can't afford private, you're afraid of the public school...
I am not "afraid"! There was that bullying-related suicide at the school and I am a concerned parent. But if you're fine with them killing themselves, let's go check it out.
They have arrived at the counter. Aidan gives the YOUNG CLERK (21) the movies. The clerk is surprised to see them.
What are you doing in here?
What are you talking about? We've been members here for 10 years.
Oh... yeah, we're closed down, man. Sorry, we're just loading what's left into boxes. Sorry I didn't see you or I would have told you we're out of business. Don't you have Netflix?
We can't afford Netflix; my husband's an actor.
Oh, I feel you. As of tomorrow I'm outta work too. The fucking future, man... it's kind of fucking us all. You can just take those though; nobody will care. (MORE)
I don't even think we've ever rented this one: "Serving Your Man, the Hasidic Way."
Aidan takes the DVD case out of the clerks hand and WINGS it frisbee style across the store.
No more Yeshiva. Tomorrow your mother and I are taking you on a tour of the public school.
INT. PUBLIC SCHOOL - DAY

Aidan, Sarah, Grace and Tucker enter to find a BIZARRELY GORGEOUS school. It's perfect: clean, beautifully lit, the kids are out of a movie. Is it a dream? A VERY HANDSOME STUDENT (14) comes towards them SINGING HIS HEART OUT to a DANCE TRACK.
(singing) We're breaking all of the rules! Oh yeah we're breaking all of the rules...
SIX INCREDIBLY FIT DANCERS join behind him and dance in beautiful seductive unison.
And we'll do what ever it takes! To fight Tim's AIDS.
He stops singing abruptly and looks at the Blooms.
Who the hell are you? And Cut!
VOICE (O.S.)
WE REVEAL that the Blooms have wandered into a film shoot taking place at the school. WE NOW NOTICE: crew members, lights, cameras.
Oh, sorry. We're looking for the... school.
An ASSISTANT DIRECTOR wearing a headset rushes over.
Sorry, Johnny. We'll get these people out of here right away.
You gotta lock this place up, man. I got an NA meeting in 15 minutes.
Yes, our fault. Sorry.
Stay in school, kids.
We're trying.
Praise Jesus.
We're not allowed to.
(to Aidan) Hello. As you can see we're filming here today. Can I help you with something?
You guys need any extra background help today? Maybe a young, wellliked teacher... I got a corduroy jacket with elbow patches in my car. Aidan!
(to AD) Can you direct us to the principal's office please?
INT. PRINCIPAL'S OFFICE - MOMENTS LATER
MRS. LUIS, AFRICAN AMERICAN (40's) sits behind her desk.
I wished you'd called ahead. We've switched to a four day school week.
Cause of budget cuts, we rent the school out on Fridays to film shoots.
Oh my God, really?
Yeah. Unfortunately a lot of schools are going the four day way. After music, arts, gym and air conditioning, what else are we gonna cut? Math?

Mrs. Luis, we can no longer afford to send our kids to the religious private school they've been attending. And as I'm sure you can imagine we have some concerns about them adjusting to the public school way of doing things.

Well I'm not gonna lie to you; this is a really tough time for Los Angeles public schools. The state's virtually bankrupt. The government's blaming the unions, the unions blame the government and in the meantime we're pooling our own money for colored pencils. (To Grace) Sweetheart, how many kids are in
Twelve. Twelve in almost all my classes except music. In music there's only 7 of us.

I just want you to manage your expectations. We're not one of the magnet schools; here at Jefferson, Grace's math class would have about 37 children in it. And as I mentioned... music has gone the way
of SAT test prep: dead as airconditioning. Sarah looks to Aidan...
INT. SCHOOL PARKING LOT - LATER
Aidan maddened; walking quickly toward the car - ahead of the rest of the family...
I'm not giving up my dream, Sarah!
Well then what's your plan, Dad?
Grace, what's the Hebrew word for charity again? Tzedakah.
WE HEAR the sounds of an OLDER MAN LAUGHING HYSTERICALLY.
INT. RABBI'S OFFICE - DAY

Rabbi Rosenberg leads Aidan into this beautifully designed lair. The sound of LAUGHTER continues as Aidan is lead through a narrow passage lined with hundreds of dusty books and Judaic tokens. Shafts of light stream through floor to ceiling stained-glass windows. They come around the corner to find the ancient RABBI TWERSKY belly laughing at a video he is watching on a laptop.
(in Hebrew) Rabbi, this is Mr. Bloom.
Mr. Bloom, Baruch Hashem. Please have a seat. Do you ever watch these Youtubes?
Uh... yeah, sure.
This kitten will not allow his brother to sleep. No matter what. I could watch it over and over again. It's the hardest I've laughed since the movie about the Greek Wedding.
(in Hebrew) Fat Greek Wedding.
Fat Girl With A Greek Wedding.
I'll have to look that one up.
Mr. Bloom, how can I help you today?
Well Rabbi, as you probably know, my father has been paying our tuition here at Hillel. Recently he has taken ill and it looks like he's not going to be able to cover us anymore.
I am deeply sorry to hear that. Your father is a lovely man. Please give him our best.
Thank you. I will.
So you're withdrawing the children from the school?
Well, no. I'd rather not...
Well, they can't go to school for free!
The two Rabbis share a hearty laugh...
We'd pay you back. It would just be for a year or so while we... you know, while my wife and I weatherout this wintery economic climate.
Rabbi Rosenberg says something to Twersky in Hebrew.
Oh yes; you're the actor.
And your wife provides for the family while you... act. Yes.
Well as both a Synagogue and Yeshiva, we exist for the community, Mr. Bloom. However, in order to do that, we must also remain a business. I'm sure you understand this.
All I'm asking for is a little...
Aidan looks down at a WORD written in his palm in Sharpie.
Twersky and Rosenberg confer briefly in HEBREW.

Mr. Bloom, if you want to buy a gallon of milk when you only have enough money for a glass of milk, you must first take that money from somewhere else. So maybe you decided that this week you will give up honey. You will go "honeyless" in order to afford a larger portion of milk.
I don't even like honey, but go on.

If I give you charity, I must take away funds from somewhere else in our limited budget to provide your two children with a free education. So how do I do that? Help me. Who, in this yeshiva, should go without honey so that the Blooms can have a gallon of free milk?
All right, look Rabbi; I'm not a religious man...
You don't say. Give me a moment to catch my breath.
But I just don't understand how you can justify not helping out a family in need in your Jewish community. I mean... wouldn't the God that you believe in say that you should help us?
God works in mysterious ways.
Yeah, I keep hearing that. I feel like that's what religious people say when they can't figure out what their God is up to.
Perhaps God is telling you to stop acting and to get a real job so you can properly provide for your family.
But what about my dream? Doesn't God believe in my "pursuit of happiness"?
No, that's the Declaration of Independence. Thomas Jefferson cared about your happiness. God wants you to provide for your family, Mr. Bloom. God believes your family deserves both milk and honey.
Both Rabbis stare down at Aidan. He races out of the office.
INT. PRAYER ROOM - MOMENTS LATER
Aidan BURSTS into a room full of bobbing and bouncing children reciting Hebrew prayers (divided into BOYS and GIRL sections by a partition). Several Rabbis turn to look...
Oh, so sorry to interrupt. Shalom everybody...
The praying STOPS COLD as all eyes turn to Aidan.
Hi, I'm Grace and Tucker's dad. Grace, I need you to come with me
On GRACE, mortified as she closes her prayer book and follows Aidan to the other side of the partition to find Tucker chewing his tzit-tzits.
Tucker! Wake-up. Gotta go bud.
Is everything okay, Mr. Bloom?
Just fine. (to the room) Once again: sorry to interrupt.
Shalom, mazel tov, sayonara. He ushers them out.
INT. MINI-VAN - LATER

It's 1982 as we watch a YOUNGER SAUL and AIDAN and JONAH as CHILDREN chase after an ICE CREAM TRUCK in SLOW MOTION. FIREFLIES FLY AROUND THEM AS THEY RUN. Elation on their faces.
EXT. SMALL OUTDOOR THEATER - SUNSET

WE SEE SPACE SUIT Costumes and we wonder for a moment if we're back in Aidan's fantasies. The CAMERA REVEALS: Aidan is directing a summer outdoor production of A Midsummer Night's Dream. The production design is straight out of Aidan's futuristic fantasies. WHITE GLOWING GLOBES like the one in his fantasy dangle from giant trees on the set. Sarah, Tucker and Grace have a picnic as they watch. FAINTLY in the background we hear the actor playing PUCK delivering his final lines:
If we shadows have offended, Think but this, and all is mended, That you have but slumber'd here While these visions did appear. And this weak and idle theme, No more yielding, but a dream
The last thing we see is a close up on Aidan's face. He lips subtly say the lines along with Puck as his eyes dance around taking it how unbelievable perfect his life has become.
